title: Rollinia exalbida
taxonomy: Rollinia exalbida is a species of fruit-bearing plant, and it belongs to the family Annonaceae. This family is commonly known as the custard apple or soursop family. Rollinia exalbida is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant's classification also includes the genus Rollinia, which is a group of flowering plants within the Annonaceae family. The exact classification of Rollinia exalbida may also include its order and kingdom, but these details are not as commonly specified.
uses: Rollinia exalbida has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments. The plant is said to possess anti-inflammatory properties, which may contribute to its medicinal uses. Its extracts have been studied for potential antiviral and antioxidant activities. However, more research is needed to confirm the effectiveness and safety of Rollinia exalbida's medicinal properties. Traditional healers in certain regions use the plant to treat fever, digestive issues, and other health conditions.

The plant is said to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to protect against cell damage and reduce inflammation in the body.
